claim: Consonant skeletons (computed by stripping vowels, word-final suffix glyphs y/n/r/m/g, and line-initial plain gallows t/p from every EVA word) carry semantic content shared across scribal hands and sections. Vowel patterns — the refinement layer examined in prior Brain-V tests — carry hand-specific metadata. If true, consonants are content; vowels are inflection/category tag.
evidence_for: H-BV-CLOSED-CLASS-01 showed zero stem overlap between Hand-A _.oii stems and Hand-B marker stems — BUT only within already-filtered vowel-pattern words. Stripped skeletons across ALL words on plant folios may tell a different story.
Brady (2026) and others have argued the Voynich may be an abjad (vowels stripped in underlying system); if consonants are content, this test operationalises that claim cleanly.

Semitic-family abjad languages universally encode content in consonants and morphology in vowels. The distinction, if present in Voynichese, would be detectable via cross-hand content clustering of consonant skeletons.
evidence_against: Medieval Latin/Occitan does not have a clean consonants-carry-content-vowels-carry-inflection structure; if the underlying language is Romance, the split might not exist

Brain-V has already shown preparation-content (pharma/recipes) markers track quire position more than hand (H-BV-TIMM-CHALLENGE-01); content may live in section-level vocabulary rather than in consonant skeletons per se
If consonants are content, cross-hand Jaccard overlap between plant folios should exceed overlap between plant and non-plant; a null result simply means consonants are not specially content-bearing
test:
For every EVA word in the corpus: strip EVA vowels {a,o,e,i}, strip a trailing suffix char from {y,n,r,m,g}, strip a line-initial plain gallows {t,p} when word-first-of-line and not bench-gallows; apply Brain-V's EVA->skeleton char map. Each folio becomes a Counter(consonant_skeleton). Metrics:
(a) Top-20 skeleton Jaccard between Hand A plant folios (sum across folios) and Hand B plant folios
(b) Top-20 skeleton Jaccard between Hand A plant folios and Hand B non-plant herbal folios
(c) Same for pharmaceutical (A) vs biological (B) vs zodiac vs (A)herbal
(d) Per-folio skeleton set similarity via mean-pairwise Jaccard within content groups vs across content groups
test_threshold: CONFIRMED if: top-20 skeleton Jaccard(HandA plant, HandB plant) >= 2x Jaccard(HandA plant, HandB non-plant herbal) AND mean per-folio Jaccard within plant content >= 2x across content. REFUTED if Jaccard ratio <= 1.2. MARGINAL between.
tests_remaining: Execute locked test in separate commit
reasoning: If this confirms, it flips Brain-V's framing: vowel markers are metadata, consonants are content. Hand A's 3-stem closed class and Hand B's 25-stem productive vocabulary would be different METADATA systems attached to the same content. If it refutes, the hands genuinely use different vocabularies at every level, and there is no shared semantic layer.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-16 pre-registration after three refutations of species-level decoding
